s58533716.txt.findings:
FINDINGS:  Frontal and lateral chest radiographs demonstrate interval clearing

lower lobes.  Within the right upper lobe, there is new faint

abscess.  There is no pleural effusion, or pneumothorax.  The cardiac
silhouette is normal in size, the mediastinal contours are notable for
unchanged calcified lymphadenopathy.  Lingular and right middle lobe
bronchiectasis is unchanged.

Findings were discussed with Dr. ___ by phone at 2:30 p.m.
s58533716.txt.impression:

increase in an ill-defined peribronchovascular opacity in the right mid lung.
This is nonspecific but might reflect an infectious or inflammatory process,
and if indicated could be better evaluated with CT.

2.  No change in bronchiectasis in the anterior right middle lobe, and
lingula.

s56694732.txt.findings:
FINDINGS:  AP single view of the chest has been obtained with patient in
semi-upright position.  Relatively high positioned diaphragm suggests poor
inspirational effort and resultant crowded appearance of pulmonary vasculature
at the bases.  There is no evidence of new discrete local parenchymal
infiltrates.  As the lateral pleural sinuses have an acute appearance,
significant pleural effusion is unlikely.  There is no evidence of
pneumothorax in the apical area as can be identified on this portable chest
examination.  When comparison is made with the next preceding PA and lateral
chest examination of ___, the findings were quite normal in this
patient who had suffered from intermittent chest tightness and palpitations.
There was no evidence of parenchymal or interstitial abnormalities.

s57739196.txt.findings:
Findings are compatible with a diffuse bronchitis/small airways disease.
______________________________________________________________________________
FINAL REPORT
INDICATION:  History: ___F with cough/wheezing/fever and diffuse rhonchi x 7
days  // ? pneumonia

TECHNIQUE:  Chest PA and lateral

COMPARISON:  ___

FINDINGS:

Since the prior radiograph from ___, there is development of
thickened airways in the left upper, left lower, and right lower lungs, which
are not accompanied by discrete areas of consolidation.  Instead, there is
increased interstitial opacification in these areas.  There is no pleural
effusion or pneumothorax.  Heart size and mediastinal contours are normal.

Findings are compatible with a diffuse bronchitis/small airways disease.
